layout_note: Single column on a bone-white page with deep aubergine/damson dark sections. The structural signature is a thin double 'paint-edge' rule (two 1px brass-tinted lines a few px apart, like a cut-in line where two colours meet) between sections, plus brass used sparingly. Boldness is spent once on the colour-confidence signature block (the boutique-feature angle) and the single warm-brass accent; everything else stays quiet. Photo-light by necessity (only one genuinely finished interior photo exists), so the page is review-led: the finished shot carries the hero, the reviews carry the colour story.

sections.copy_intent: Text wordmark 'AC Painters & Decorators'; nav: Work, Reviews, Areas; one button 'Get a quote'. No phone in the bar on mobile.
sections.visual_intent: Quiet solid bone-white header (no backdrop-filter so the mobile drawer/scrim are not trapped - containing-block rule), paint-edge hairline underneath. Mobile: wordmark + hamburger only (no phone, <=2 links) per mobile_header check.

sections.purpose: State trade + location + the colour-confidence angle in one line; show the one real finished interior; surface the only citable proof.
evidence_anchors: reviews[2] Sylvia (olive green, boutique hotel look)
10-places.webp (the only finished, well-lit interior: smooth deep-grey stairwell, crisp black flush door, white spindles - reads boutique/moody, on-brand for the aubergine palette)
sections.copy_intent: Headline = trade-led positioning on the colour-confidence / feature-finish angle (NOT a review quote). Subhead = interior and exterior painting and decorating across Bootle, Crosby and Liverpool, with the bold-colour/feature-finish line + 4.9/58 proof. CTAs: Get a quote (primary, #quote), Call (secondary, phone).
sections.visual_intent: Full-bleed 10-places.webp under a deep-aubergine (#2E1A2C) scrim for legibility; Bodoni Moda editorial headline (boutique-fashion feel), warm-brass primary button; small proof chip '4.9 on Google, 58 reviews'. Text block keeps the site container side padding on mobile (hero horizontal-inset rule); CTAs stack full-width <=640px, proof chip below both.

sections.purpose: Evidence-drawn proof points directly under the hero.
sections.copy_intent: 4-6 factual points: 4.9 on Google, 58 local reviews, Bootle and Crosby L20, interior and exterior, feature-colour finishes, tidy and on time. No exclamation marks, no salesy framing.
sections.visual_intent: Deep-aubergine band, bone text, brass diamond separators; single linear 36s loop, content rendered twice, translateX(-50%); animates on all viewports (reduced-motion degrades to a static row).

sections.purpose: The one memorable thing: this team is confident with colour - the feature wall, the boutique finish, furniture and shelving brought back, inside and out. This is where boldness is spent.
evidence_anchors: reviews[2] Sylvia (olive green, boutique hotel look, dated shelves unit transformed)
reviews[1] N (bedroom paint + wallpaper, excellent finish)
reviews[0] nic (finish high quality, exactly what I was after)
sections.copy_intent: 3 short, concrete statements of the specialism, stated plainly: colour done with confidence (the feature wall / boutique finish), furniture and shelving refinished not just walls, and exterior work on terrace and period houses. Each tied to a real job. No boast adjectives in site prose.
sections.visual_intent: Mid-aubergine (--plum) feature surface; large Bodoni Moda statements separated by paint-edge rules; brass used once on the key phrase ('a luxurious, boutique-hotel finish'). No icons. The boldest moment on the page.

sections.purpose: Name the 4 real services from evidence with specific, grounded descriptions.
evidence_anchors: site-strategy.json real_services (feature colour & full-room painting, wallpaper hanging, furniture & shelving refinishing, exterior house painting)
sections.copy_intent: Each service: short label + one evidence-grounded line. NEVER the repeated Google niche label 'Painting & decorating' x3. NEVER 'X services explained plainly'.
sections.visual_intent: Labelled rows with small two-letter marks (FC / WP / FR / EX) in brass - NOT 01/02/03 (services are not a sequence; the numbered process below is) and NOT SVG/lucide icons (no_service_icons check). Paint-edge rule between rows.

sections.purpose: Show the real finished work that exists - honestly. Only one genuinely finished interior photo is available; the period staircase shows the kind of older-house decorating they take on. Captions stay honest and never claim a finish a photo does not show.
evidence_anchors: brief.json photos[] (10-places.webp finished grey stairwell; 03-places.webp period staircase mid-renovation)
sections.copy_intent: Short section intro. Captions safe and honest: finished shot -> 'Stairwell and landing, Liverpool'; period staircase (freshly plastered, stripped treads) -> framed honestly as period-house work, e.g. 'Period staircase, Liverpool'. Do NOT label a mid-renovation shot as 'finished'.
sections.visual_intent: CSS masonry (2 tiles): natural portrait aspect ratios, top-aligned, captions directly under each. Thin brass-tinted frame on each tile. 02-places.webp (room strip-out with a hire dehumidifier and bare plaster, no finished surface) is EXCLUDED per the hero/gallery rule (WIP / no finished surface).

sections.purpose: Exactly one review section. This is where the colour story lives - lead with Sylvia's boutique-finish quote and Helen's Crosby exterior.
sections.copy_intent: 4 verbatim Google excerpts (emoji stripped from quote text), attributed 'First name, Google review'. Sylvia, Helen, N (kept as 'Google review' since 'N' is an initial, not a usable first name), nic. No first name as a headline (no 'Steve' anti-pattern). One review section only.
sections.visual_intent: Bone (--stone) cards; brass quote rule; the 4.9/58 proof restated once as a stat, label 'Google rating' (no banned sub-labels). Exactly one review section.

sections.purpose: Reduce risk: show the orderly, tidy way the team works.
evidence_anchors: reviews[0] nic (came out promptly to quote, turned up on time, kept everything tidy)
reviews[1] N (fit me in for a quote, communication good, cleaned up after)
sections.copy_intent: 4 ordered steps grounded in review behaviour: come out to quote (often at short notice) -> agree the colours and finish -> paint or paper to a high finish -> clean up and leave it tidy. Numbering is honest here (a real sequence).
sections.visual_intent: Deep-aubergine (--ink) dark section for contrast; large brass Bodoni numerals 01-04; paint-edge dividers between steps.

sections.purpose: Establish locality and availability without exposing the street address.
sections.copy_intent: Based in Bootle, covers Crosby, Liverpool and north Merseyside (8-14 districts). Crosby evidenced; rest derived from L20 (Bootle) geography (plausible small-team radius, no over-claim). Hours: open seven days, from early. No street address.
sections.visual_intent: Keyless Google Maps iframe centred on 'Bootle L20' (town + outward only); two-column area list + hours beside it.

sections.purpose: Convert: a quote form plus phone, anchored at #quote.
sections.copy_intent: Two columns. Left: heading + framing prose + key details (phone click-to-call, Bootle / L20, open seven days). Right: quote form (name, phone, postcode, job type, details). Job types are painter-specific. Heading specific to AC Painters. Form is presentational only (no submit to the business; no 'preview form' disclaimer).
sections.visual_intent: Deep-aubergine (#2E1A2C) dark surface, NOT the brass accent (contact section rule). Form fields in a light bone field, visually distinct from the side details. Mobile: stack to single column, details first. Quote-only sticky CTA reveals after hero on scroll (fade+slide), hides near footer.

sections.purpose: Real local-business footer, not just the agency credit.
sections.copy_intent: Brand, phone, areas, hours, quick links, link to read Google reviews. Small 'Website by WebForTrades' credit at the bottom.

omitted_defaults: owner-backstory/about-me narrative (no self-description evidence; only the first name Chris + 'the lads')
fabricated stats band beyond Google 4.9/58 + seven-days availability
an owner photo or logo (none found)
a large multi-image masonry (only one finished photo exists; padding it would be dishonest)
omitted_reasons: Kept lean and evidence-only. No section exists without a specific anchor in the frozen brief. The gallery is deliberately small because only one genuinely finished photo is available; the colour story is carried by the signature block and the verbatim reviews instead.
